anchor.snippet: a plane is a flat two- dimensional surface that extends indefinitely
A plane segment or planar region
A bivector is an oriented plane segment
A face is a plane segment bounding a solid object
A slab is a region bounded by two parallel planes
A parallelepiped is a region bounded by three pairs of parallel planes
equipped with a chosen Cartesian coordinate system is called a Cartesian plane
a non-Cartesian Euclidean plane equipped with a polar coordinate system would be called a polar plane
A plane is a ruled surface
a Euclidean plane is a Euclidean space of dimension two
the ordered pairs of real numbers (the real coordinate plane ), equipped with the dot product , is often called the Euclidean plane or standard Euclidean plane

anchor.section: Determination by contained points and lines
anchor.snippet: a plane is uniquely determined by any of the following

annotations.label: Two planes parallel or intersect in a line

anchor.snippet: Two distinct planes are either parallel or they intersect in a line
A line is either parallel to a plane, intersects it at a single point, or is contained in the plane

annotations.label: Lines perpendicular to same plane are parallel

anchor.snippet: Two distinct lines perpendicular to the same plane must be parallel to each other

annotations.label: Planes perpendicular to same line are parallel

anchor.snippet: Two distinct planes perpendicular to the same line must be parallel to each other

anchor.section: Distance from a point to a plane
anchor.snippet: the distance from a point to a plane is the distance between a given point and its orthogonal projection on the plane

anchor.section: Line of intersection between two planes

When the intersection of a sphere and a plane is not empty or a single point, it is a circle

annotations.label: Unique circle through three points on a sphere

anchor.snippet: on a sphere there is exactly one circle that can be drawn through three given points

annotations.label: Points of a circle equidistant from its poles

anchor.snippet: the points on a circle are all a common angular distance from one of its poles
The attitude of a lattice plane is the orientation of the line normal to the plane
The trend is the compass direction of the line
A strike line is the intersection of a horizontal plane with the observed planar feature
